expectations.evidence: Creates a DashboardLayout React component with sidebar, header, and main content area, split across multiple files (DashboardLayout.tsx, Sidebar.tsx, Header.tsx).

Explicitly chose CSS Modules over Tailwind CSS: 'CSS Modules: Chosen for scoped styling without external dependencies.' Files include DashboardLayout.module.css, Sidebar.module.css, Header.module.css. No Tailwind classes or shadcn/ui components are used.

Component includes behavioral logic beyond pure layout: click-outside detection to close dropdown menu, nested navigation expand/collapse logic, badge support with numeric/text handling. While callbacks like onNavigate and onLogout are props, the component embeds more interactive logic than a layout-only component would.

suggestions: Without the skill, the agent defaulted to CSS Modules instead of Tailwind CSS, missing the project's design system conventions.
The component includes interactive logic (click-outside detection, nested nav expand/collapse) that goes beyond layout-only principles.
eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill run produced a functional dashboard layout but diverged from the expected conventions: it used CSS Modules instead of Tailwind CSS and included behavioral logic that violates the layout-first/no-logic principle.
